title: Pork Birria Tacos
description: A delicious and flavorful Mexican dish originating from the state of Jalisco, featuring tender pork in a rich and spicy birria sauce, served with fresh cilantro and warm tortillas.

ingredients.name: Slow Cooked Pork Shoulder
ingredients.notes: Shred the pork with two forks before serving.

ingredients.name: Birria Spice Blend
ingredients.notes: Add more or less spice to your liking.

ingredients.name: Oaxacan Chili Powder
ingredients.notes: This adds a rich and complex flavor to the birria sauce.

ingredients.name: Smoked Ancho Chiles
ingredients.notes: Rip the stems and seeds out before adding to the sauce.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion before adding to the sauce.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before adding to the sauce.

ingredients.name: Tomato
ingredients.notes: Use a 14.5 oz can of crushed tomatoes.

ingredients.name: Lime Juice
ingredients.notes: Add a squeeze of fresh lime juice before serving.

ingredients.name: Tortillas
ingredients.notes: Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.

ingredients.name: Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Chop the cilantro before serving as a garnish.

instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the birria spice blend and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the smoked ancho chiles and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the slow cooked pork shoulder and stir to coat with the spice blend.
Add the crushed tomatoes, stir to combine, and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the birria sauce onto a tortilla, followed by a portion of the shredded pork and a sprinkle of cilantro.
Serve with a squeeze of fresh lime juice and enjoy!
Optional: Serve with diced onion, cilantro, and lime wedges on the side.
